findings.evidence: `nl -ba` shows `def fourierAtom` at lines 58-59, `fourierAtom_eq_fourierChar ... := rfl` at lines 68-70, and `theorem fourierAtom_def` rewriting through `Real.fourierChar_apply` at lines 74-77; Mathlib has `theorem fourierChar_apply` in `Mathlib/Analysis/Complex/Circle.lean:219`.
findings.fix: Rename this theorem to an application/evaluation name such as `fourierAtom_apply` or a conclusion-specific name such as `fourierAtom_eq_exp`, and update uses/docstrings accordingly.
findings.issue: `fourierAtom_def` names the raw exponential expansion as a definition, but `fourierAtom` is defined at line 58 as `(Real.fourierChar (-(inner ℝ v q)) : ℂ)` and line 68 already provides the rfl definitional theorem `fourierAtom_eq_fourierChar`; Mathlib names the corresponding exponential expansion `Real.fourierChar_apply`, not `_def`.

summary: One exported theorem name mislabels an exponential rewrite as the definition even though the definition is the `Real.fourierChar` form.
